calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2023 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Limited Scope Attorney Michael Yesks Ccp 473 Motion For Relief From Order
Off Calendar Apr-05-2023 Advanced From Apr-26-23 To Apr-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Limited Scope Attorney Michael Yesks Ccp 473 Motion For Relief From Order
Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Allegre Aguirre, Esq. 714-923-3369 WFBMLLP for DEFENDANT MM CLUB L.L.C.
No other Appearance.

THE PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTY AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - The matter is Not contested/Discussed. JPT Deol did not receive any contest email. Counsel appears in an abundance of caution since there is a party appearing on behalf of Plainiff who is not an attorney. The Court adopts the tentative ruling and will sign the proposed order submitted.

2-PLAINTIFF KEYHAN MOHANNA, INDIVIDUALLY, AND AS TRUSTEE's Motion Of Limited Scope Attorney Michael Yesks Ccp 473 Motion For Relief From Order.

The motion for relief under CCP 473(b) is granted. The court hereby relieves Mr. Yesk (but not Plaintiff) of any obligation to pay monetary sanctions under its order dated December 22, 2022. Furthermore, to be clear, the court never intended that the order to pay sanctions apply to limited-scope counsel, Mr. Yesk, in the first place.

Mr. Yesk submits a proposed order to Judge Pro Tem Deol at jpdeol@dhillonlaw.com for signature.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

1-DEFENDANT MM CLUB L.L.C.'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, ISSUE SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF KEYHAN MOHANNA. (FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY, PART 2 OF 2)

Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Off Calendar

Defendant Mm Club, Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Keyhan Mohanna; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Allegre Aguirre, Esq. 714-923-3369 WFBM LLP for DEFENDANT MM CLUB L.L.C.
No other Appearance.

THE PARTY STIPULATES TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTY AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

RULING - The matter is Not contested/Discussed. Counsel appears in an abundance of caution since there is a party appearing on behalf of Plainiff who is not an attorney. The Court adopts the tentative ruling and will sign the proposed order submitted.

1-DEFENDANT MM CLUB L.L.C.'s Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Keyhan Mohanna.

The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Terminating and issue/evidentiary sanctions are denied without prejudice. Plaintiff is given a final chance to comply with his obligations to respond (without objections) to Defendant's discovery requests, as ordered by the court on December 22, 2022, with the exception of the requests for admission (see below regarding the requests for admission specifically). Plaintiff has fourteen (14) calendar days to comply fully. Absent full compliance, Defendant may file and serve another motion for terminating sanctions, which will likely be granted. Due to Plaintiff's failure to respond to Defendant's first set of requests for admission, the truth of all matters specified therein is hereby deemed admitted under CCP 2033.280(b). Plaintiff (and only Plaintiff, not his limited-scope counsel) is ordered to pay the sanctions awarded in the court's December 22, 2022 order, in the amount of $3,960.00, within fourteen (14) calendar days, by delivering a check for that amount to the office of Defendant's counsel. Monetary sanctions related to this current motion are awarded against Plaintiff (and only against Plaintiff, not against his limited-scope counsel) in the amount of $2,800.00. Plaintiff is to deliver a separate check for that amount to the office of Defendant's counsel within fourteen (14) calendar days. Defendant is to serve on Plaintiff and to file of a notice of entry of this order. Plaintiff is further ordered to file a declaration stating that he has complied fully with the terms of this order within twenty-one (21) calendar days of Defendant's service and filing a notice of entry of this order.

Defendant's counsel submits a proposed order to Judge Pro Tem Deol at jpdeol@dhillonlaw.com for signature.

Judge Pro Tem John-Paul Deol, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Jul-15-2022 Text Ruling

JOHN A. LOVEMAN, ESQ., RAFF LAW, APC, 707-879-8040, jloveman@rafflawoffice.com, for Defendant
MM CLUB L.L.C. appearing via ZOOM,
No Other appearances,

Counsel Michael Yesk appears for plaintiff but is not allowed to argue. A Substitution of Attorney or Limted Scope Appearance was not filed on Plaintiff in pro per's behalf.

Contested. Matter is discussed. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDNAT MM CLUB L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for elder abuse which is sufficiently stated. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for cancellation of instruments, which is barred by res judicata. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for fraud/civil conspiracy for Plaintiff to allege facts with requisite particularity to support each element of this cause of action. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file and serve and amend complaint. If an amended complaint is not filed, Defendant shall file an answer to the current complaint (subject to this ruling) within 12 days of notice of entry of order. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
